At the court's query, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petition. At conclusion of argument and additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Petition For Surcharge, For Removal Of The Administrator, And For Appointment Of A Successor Administrator
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Continued To Apr-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Lisa D. Tandoc, Esq. for Jackie Joubert (Petitioner) also via Zoom. Steven Schroeder, Esq. for Inheritance Funding Company (Assignee/Objector). Merily Sanchez, Esq. for No. America Specialty Insurance Company. Amber Joubert (Interested Party). Wardy Joubert (Interested Party).

The Court stated before it today is the First Account and Final Report of the (Suspended) Administrator. Mr. Schroeder argued that Ms. Joubert converted assets that belonged to the estate, that she did not file a claim, and that they are owed $129,500.00. Mr. Schroeder further argued that six years later they have not been paid, that Ms. Joubert breached her fiduciary duty, that there should be a surcharge, and that pursuant to Probate Code 859 there should be additional damages. The Court stated that it won't consider a surcharge without a petition, that there is a need for a successor administrator, that someone has to be appointed, and that maybe the creditor can serve. The Court issued its tentative ruling stating that it intends to acknowledge the accounting and then add a status hearing on the filing of a petition for surcharge, for removal of administrator, and to appoint a successor administrator to the calendar. Ms. Tandoc argued that there was to be a 50/50 split, referenced exhibit four to her petition, stated that 50% ownership of the property belonged to her client, that there were bankruptcy proceedings, and that the second title company didn't acknowledge the bankruptcy court. The Court stated that this is about the assignment and if Ms. Joubert owes Inheritance Funding Company.

At the conclusion of argument, the First Account and Final Report of the (Suspended) Administrator is taken under submission. A status hearing on the filing of a petition for surcharge, for removal of administrator, and to appoint a successor administrator is set for March 14, 2023, at 9;00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

dge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: J. Choi. Matter not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lisa Tandoc, via Zoom, for Jackie Joubert, via Zoom, Administrator; Amber Joubert, via Zoom, in propria persona, Beneficiary; Wardy Joubert, IV, via Zoom, in propria persona, Beneficiary; Steven Schroeder, via Zoom, for Inheritance Funding, Assignee.

The court and Ms. Joubert discuss the status of the proceeds of the sale of the real property with Ms. Joubert confirming that she primarily used the proceeds to care for her children and to avoid foreclosure. Ms. Joubert confirms that she was the listing agent and made a commission from the sale. The court points Ms. Joubert to not only the issue of using estate proceeds without court approval but also of the conflict of interest of her serving as listing agent. Ms. Joubert confirms her address and that her children live with her. Both Amber Joubert and Wardy Joubert, IV, offer testimony and confirm their understanding that their mother used the estate funds to purchase a home and to care for them. The court and Ms. Tandoc discuss the status of an accounting with Ms. Tandoc reporting that an accounting should be filed by the next status hearing date of May-15-2022. Ms. Tandoc confirms that there no estate assets remain, and the court confirms that it's not prudent to reappoint Jackie Joubert as Administrator or to appoint an Administrator Ad Litem, accordingly. The court and Mr. Schroeder discuss his client's position regarding the recovery of the funds issued via assignments. The court advises Mr. Schroeder that he should consider filing a petition for surcharge and if he does, he should make certain to serve notice on the bonding company.

The Status Petition is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Obitinalla. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-23-2022 Continued To Apr-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Lisa Tandoc, via zoom, for Jackie Joubert, Administrator; Steven Schroeder, via zoom, for Inheritance Funding Co., Inc., Assignee.

The court acknowledges Ms. Tandoc's recent filing of a Report on Status of Administration and advises her that it appears that the document should have been filed as an amended Report or as a supplement which would have required the filing of a notice of hearing, thereon. Ms. Tandoc provides oral testimony regarding the status of this matter including, but not limited to, a bankruptcy proceeding against the Administrator. The court comments that it is concerned that it was not advised of the bankruptcy proceeding as it likely would not have appointed Jackie Joubert as Administrator. Ms. Tandoc reports that she does not have a specific date but that the bankruptcy had been discharged prior to the initial filing of the estate sometime in 2017. The court and Ms. Tandoc discuss the issue of the uncertainty of whether the decedent held a 50% or a 100% interest in the title of the real property and advises that the Administrator's position regarding the preceding must be confirmed before this matter can proceed. The court confirms with Ms. Tandoc of the uncertainty regarding whether the proceeds from the sale of the real property have been deposited into an account under the name of the estate. The court advises Ms. Tandoc that it is ordering a two-year accounting from Dec-11-2016 to Dec-10-2018. After additional discussion, the court advises in open court that it is suspending the Administrator and orders the following:

1) Administrator shall file Proof of deposit of proceeds from the sale of real property into a bank account under the name of the estate by Mar-30-2022.

2) Administrator shall file the closing documents regarding the sale of real property by Mar-30-2022.

3) Administrator is to file a declaration in support of status with a date of Apr-20-2022 which is to be filed by Apr-06-2022.

4) The Administrator is ordered to appear on Apr-20-2022.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Status is continued to Apr-20-2022 at 9:00 am. A Status of Two-Year Accounting and Status on Close of Estate are set for May-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. If an Accounting Petition and Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration are filed by May-11-2022, both status hearings will be taken off calendar.

Mr. Schroeder advises that he will submit a proposed order regarding today's hearing to the assigned examiner via email, cc'ing Ms. Tandoc.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Continued To Jan-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company.

The Court noted that there is no appearance by Ms. Tandoc. Mr. Schroeder stated that he has not spoken with Ms. Tandoc since April, that she was difficult to reach, that the last status report indicates that the house was sold, that $400,000.00 was left over from the sale and that the Administrator needs to account for it. Mr. Schroeder also stated that Inheritance Funding is owed $129,500.00. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on January 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, why a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed, and to show why the Administrator should not be removed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
